name: Hot and Sour Soup 4
recipeInstructions:
1. Partially freeze pork; cut into 1 x 1/4-inch strips. Sprinkle meat with 1 tablespoon of soy sauce.
2. Soak dried lily buds, mushrooms, and tree ears in hot water to cover for 30 minutes. Drain them.
3. Cut off and discard the tough stem end of lily buds. Slice each lily bud into 1-inch lengths.
4. Rinse tree ears under running water; drain well. Discard stems.
5. Cut off and discard stems from mushrooms and tree ears. Cut into thin strips.
6. In a large saucepan, bring chicken broth to boiling. Stir in pork, remaining soy sauce, lily buds, mushrooms, tree ears, rice wine vinegar, and pepper. Simmer covered for 10 minutes.
7. Slice tofu into 1/4-inch-wide strips. Add to soup and simmer, covered, for an additional 3 minutes.
8. Blend cold water into cornstarch. Stir into soup.
9. Pour beaten egg slowly into hot soup in a thin stream, beating constantly with a fork until the egg cooks and shreds finely.
10. Cook soup until slightly thickened and bubbly. Remove from heat. Stir in thinly sliced green onion.
